evidence_slots.subject: external debug interface requester on top-level debug_req_i
evidence_slots.operation: enter debug mode / redirect PC to debug handler

evidence_slots.expected_guard: debug entry should be mediated by debug authorization/lifecycle policy and should not be asserted solely by an external request; entry should also respect pipeline/AMO safety conditions
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: ariane masks debug_req_i only with ~amo_valid_commit. csr_regfile debug logic can set dpc_d=pc_i, debug_mode_d=1, set_debug_pc_o=1 on an external debug request while not already in debug mode. debug_mode_q then selects dm::ExceptionAddress and causes privileged debug execution semantics. No authentication, lifecycle, lock, or debug-enable policy signal is visible in the inspected path.
evidence_slots.path: src/ariane.sv debug_req_i -> debug_req -> csr_regfile.debug_req_i -> debug_mode_q/debug_mode_o and set_debug_pc_o -> ariane set_debug_pc/debug_mode -> frontend.set_debug_pc_i/debug_mode_i and controller.set_debug_pc_i
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset clears debug_mode_q; ariane assign debug_req = debug_req_i & ~amo_valid_commit; debug set occurs when not in debug mode and debug_req_i is accepted with a valid commit instruction; dret clears debug_mode_d. debug mode suppresses counters/interrupts and forces priv_lvl_o to M.
evidence_slots.impact: An external debug requester can force debug mode and redirect execution to the debug exception address without an RTL-visible authorization guard.

controller receives set_debug_pc_i(set_debug_pc).

if debug_mode_q, trap_vector_base_o = dm::ExceptionAddress.
dret returns from debug and clears debug_mode_d.
debug mode affects priv_lvl_o, mprv, and debug_mode_o.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: External debug_req_i can drive debug entry and PC redirection with only AMO-commit masking visible; no debug authorization/lifecycle policy is present in the inspected RTL.
inspection_records.notes: AMO safety condition is partially satisfied by top-level masking, but expected authorization/lifecycle mediation is absent.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible CSR instruction interface
evidence_slots.operation: read/write debug CSRs through normal CSR instruction interface
evidence_slots.object: CSR_DCSR, CSR_DPC, CSR_DSCRATCH0, CSR_DSCRATCH1; dcsr_q, dpc_q, dscratch0_q, dscratch1_q
evidence_slots.expected_guard: debug CSRs should be readable/writable only in debug mode or by authorized debug context; non-debug U/S/M CSR instructions should trap without updating debug backing state
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: The read mux returns dcsr_q/dpc_q/dscratch values for 0x7b0-0x7b3. The write case directly assigns dcsr_d, dpc_d, dscratch0_d, dscratch1_d for the same addresses. exception_ctrl separately asserts illegal instruction for csr_addr_i[11:4] == 8'h7b when !debug_mode_q; however, the observed update case is not locally conditional on debug_mode_q.
evidence_slots.path: csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/csr_op_i -> csr_we/csr_rdata -> debug CSR read/write cases -> dcsr_d/dpc_d/dscratch*_d -> sequential debug CSR registers; separate csr_exception_o for !debug_mode_q debug CSR access
evidence_slots.state_condition: debug_mode_q false causes csr_exception_o.valid for 0x7b* accesses, but write decode for 0x7b0-0x7b3 is unconditional under csr_we. debug_mode_q true makes priv_lvl_o M.
evidence_slots.impact: If illegal-instruction handling does not squash same-cycle CSR state updates, normal software may read or modify protected debug CSR state outside debug mode.

rtl_evidence.text: CSR_DCSR=12'h7b0, CSR_DPC=12'h7b1, CSR_DSCRATCH0=12'h7b2, CSR_DSCRATCH1=12'h7b3.
read mux returns DCSR, DPC, DSCRATCH0, DSCRATCH1.
write case updates dcsr_d, dpc_d, dscratch0_d, dscratch1_d.
debug-mode-only CSR exception for 0x7b* if !debug_mode_q.
sequential update of debug_mode_q, dcsr_q, dpc_q, dscratch0_q, dscratch1_q.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Debug CSR backing state is decoded in the normal CSR read/write path; non-debug access raises an illegal-instruction sideband, but the update cases themselves are not gated by debug_mode_q.
inspection_records.notes: The presence of csr_exception_o is a guard only if downstream commit/squash semantics prevent the sequential update; that squash behavior was not evidenced in the provided observations.

evidence_slots.subject: software CSR access path from decoded instructions

evidence_slots.expected_guard: machine CSRs and privilege-control fields should be writable only by machine privilege or equivalent trap/exception mediation; U/S writes must be rejected and must not update backing state

The privilege check is not used to gate the update assignments and includes a special exception excluding CSR_MEPC from illegal-instruction generation.

evidence_slots.subject: software CSR access path / lower privilege modes
evidence_slots.operation: configure address translation policy through SATP and SFENCE/VMA-related controls
evidence_slots.object: CSR_SATP, satp mode/asid/ppn state, en_translation_o, en_ld_st_translation_o, satp_ppn_o, asid_o, flush_tlb_ctrl_ex
evidence_slots.expected_guard: SATP writes and translation-control changes should be allowed only from permitted S/M privilege and should be blocked/trapped when machine TVM policy forbids S-mode SATP/SFENCE changes
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: csr_regfile read mux exposes SATP and write case updates satp_d from csr_wdata when mode is MODE_OFF or MODE_SV39, with ASID masking. No TVM check is visible in the SATP write case. tvm_o is output from mstatus_q.tvm and routed to id_stage, suggesting ID may mediate SFENCE/TVM, but the provided RTL evidence does not show TVM gating of csr_regfile SATP updates. Translation outputs are directly derived from satp_q and priv_lvl_o.
evidence_slots.path: csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/csr_op_i -> CSR_SATP write case -> satp_d/satp_q -> satp_ppn_o/asid_o/en_translation_o/en_ld_st_translation_o -> execute/MMU path; tvm_o -> ariane tvm_csr_id -> id_stage.tvm_i
evidence_slots.state_condition: S-mode access to SATP is allowed by CSR address privilege encoding, but when mstatus.tvm is set, S-mode SATP/SFENCE should trap. The observed SATP update case has no tvm_o/mstatus_q.tvm condition. id_stage receives tvm_i, but its detailed handling is not in the provided observations.
evidence_slots.impact: S-mode software may be able to change address translation state despite TVM policy if ID-stage trapping does not fully prevent the CSR update; this affects memory isolation through PPN/ASID/translation-enable outputs.

inspection_records.notes: TVM handling may exist in id_stage, but no evidence was provided showing it gates the SATP CSR update itself.

evidence_slots.subject: software CSR access path
evidence_slots.operation: change memory/privilege access policy through status CSRs
evidence_slots.object: mstatus/sstatus permission bits SUM, MXR, MPRV, TVM, TW, TSR and related outputs
evidence_slots.expected_guard: writes to fields that relax memory or supervisor controls should be restricted to sufficiently privileged modes and preserve field-level architectural masking
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: SSTATUS read/write uses SMODE_STATUS_READ_MASK/WRITE_MASK, but mask definitions were not present in the provided evidence. MSTATUS write assigns mstatus_d = csr_wdata, then only hardwires some fields such as sd/sxl/uxl/fs. Policy outputs sum_o, mxr_o, tvm_o, tw_o, tsr_o, and mprv are driven from mstatus_q. Lower-privilege blocking relies on the generic sideband privilege exception, not on write-case gating.
evidence_slots.path: csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/csr_op_i -> CSR_SSTATUS/CSR_MSTATUS write cases -> mstatus_d/mstatus_q -> sum_o/mxr_o/tvm_o/tw_o/tsr_o/mprv/en_ld_st_translation_o -> ID/EX/MMU
evidence_slots.state_condition: SSTATUS write applies SMODE_STATUS_WRITE_MASK; MSTATUS write raw-loads csr_wdata. mret/sret also mutate status stack fields. Generic privilege exception is asserted later if address privilege check fails, with CSR_MEPC exception unrelated to status but showing sideband style.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthorized or improperly masked writes to mstatus policy bits can relax memory access checks (SUM/MXR/MPRV) or alter S-mode restrictions (TVM/TW/TSR).

rtl_evidence.text: mstatus field definitions include tsr, tw, tvm, mxr, sum, mprv, interrupt stack fields.
SSTATUS read returns mstatus_q & SMODE_STATUS_READ_MASK.
SSTATUS write applies SMODE_STATUS_WRITE_MASK, updates mstatus_d, and flushes.
MSTATUS write assigns mstatus_d=csr_wdata and hardwires selected fields.
mret/sret update privilege stack bits.
sum_o, mxr_o, tvm_o, tw_o, tsr_o, mprv outputs derive from mstatus_q/debug state.
ex_stage receives translation, privilege, SUM, MXR, SATP fields.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Definitions of SMODE_STATUS_READ_MASK and SMODE_STATUS_WRITE_MASK were not found in the provided observations.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Status policy fields drive MMU/privilege outputs; SSTATUS uses a mask, but MSTATUS accepts raw csr_wdata and update gating depends on sideband privilege exceptions rather than local write guards.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate violation is driven by raw MSTATUS update and sideband-only privilege enforcement; SSTATUS masking may be correct but was not fully evidenced.

evidence_slots.subject: U/S/M CSR requester through software CSR instruction path

Performance CSR writes set perf_data_o=csr_wdata and perf_we_o=1. SCOUNTEREN reads as 0 and write is a no-op; MCOUNTEREN is defined but no implementation evidence was observed. evidence_slots.impact: Lower-privilege software can observe timing/performance events and possibly configure performance counters, leaking execution activity or enabling side-channel measurement beyond intended policy.

evidence_slots.subject: software CSR access path
evidence_slots.object: CSR_ICACHE, CSR_DCACHE, icache_q, dcache_q, icache_en_o, dcache_en_o, icache_flush_ctrl_cache, dcache_flush_ctrl_cache
evidence_slots.expected_guard: cache enable/disable and cache flush controls should be writable only by privileged software and not by U-mode or unauthorized aliases
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Custom CSR_ICACHE/CSR_DCACHE addresses are defined at 0x700/0x701. csr_regfile read mux returns icache_q/dcache_q, outputs icache_en_o/dcache_en_o derive from those registers, and ariane routes them to the cache subsystem. Search evidence shows flush_o side effects near write cases, consistent with CSR side effects. The provided observations do not show a cache-specific guard; enforcement appears to depend on generic CSR privilege exception logic, whose exception is sideband to state update.
evidence_slots.path: csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/csr_op_i -> CSR_ICACHE/CSR_DCACHE write cases -> icache_q/dcache_q -> icache_en_o/dcache_en_o -> ariane cache subsystem; csr flush_o -> controller flush_icache/flush_dcache controls
evidence_slots.state_condition: Lower-privilege request should fail based on CSR address privilege decode for 0x700/0x701, but no local cache-write guard is evidenced. icache_en_o is additionally suppressed in debug mode; dcache_en_o is not suppressed by debug mode.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthorized cache enable/disable or flush can affect memory-system behavior, performance, coherency, or isolation if sideband CSR exceptions do not suppress the write side effect.

csr_regfile outputs dcache_en_o and icache_en_o into ariane signals.

cache subsystems receive icache_en_i, dcache_enable_i, icache_flush_i, dcache_flush_i.
generic privilege check only raises csr_exception_o, not a local write enable gate.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The exact CSR_ICACHE/CSR_DCACHE write-case lines were not fully visible, but the fact layer and search observations show corresponding backing state, read paths, outputs, and side-effect flush locations.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Custom cache-control CSRs drive cache enable/flush behavior; write mediation relies on generic CSR privilege sideband rather than local update gating.
inspection_records.notes: Because exact custom write decode text is partially truncated, this record is candidate rather than conclusive.

evidence_slots.expected_guard: mret/sret/dret should be permitted only in corresponding privilege/debug state, and should restore PC/privilege from the correct protected EPC register; sret should respect TSR
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: csr_op_logic sets mret, sret, or dret for MRET/SRET/DRET opcodes and suppresses normal CSR read/write side effects, but no current-privilege or debug_mode_q check is visible in that block. Return-update logic unconditionally performs eret_o and updates priv_lvl_d/status/debug_mode_d when the corresponding decoded signal is true. epc_o selects mepc_q by default, sepc_q if sret, and dpc_q if dret. Generic privilege checking applies only when csr_we or csr_read is true, so it does not cover return ops after csr_we/csr_read are cleared. id_stage receives priv_lvl_i/tvm_i/tw_i/tsr_i/debug_mode_i and may reject illegal returns, but that evidence is outside the provided RTL observations.
evidence_slots.path: decoded system return instruction -> csr_op_i MRET/SRET/DRET -> mret/sret/dret -> eret_o/epc_o/priv_lvl_d/debug_mode_d -> ariane frontend/controller PC redirection
evidence_slots.state_condition: Return signals are cleared if ex_i.valid. For mret: eret_o asserted, priv_lvl_d=mstatus_q.mpp. For sret: eret_o asserted, priv_lvl_d={1'b0,mstatus_q.spp}. For dret: eret_o asserted, priv_lvl_d=dcsr_q.prv, debug_mode_d=0. epc_o selects DPC whenever dret is true, regardless of debug_mode_q in the shown block.
evidence_slots.impact: If decode-stage legality checks do not prevent these CSR ops, lower modes or non-debug code can redirect PC from protected EPC/DPC and alter privilege/debug state.

rtl_evidence.text: mret/sret/dret update eret_o, privilege stack fields, priv_lvl_d, and debug_mode_d.
csr_op_logic sets sret/mret/dret for SRET/MRET/DRET and clears csr_we/csr_read.
ex_i.valid clears mret/sret/dret.
generic CSR privilege check runs only if csr_we || csr_read, so return ops are not checked here after csr_we/csr_read are cleared.
epc_o defaults to mepc_q, selects sepc_q on sret and dpc_q on dret.
frontend receives epc_i and eret_i for PC generation.
id_stage receives priv_lvl_i, debug_mode_i, tvm_i, tw_i, tsr_i.
controller receives eret_i and controls PC/flush behavior.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: ID-stage legality handling for MRET/SRET/DRET and TSR was not included in the provided observations.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Return operations mret/sret/dret are accepted by csr_op_logic without local privilege/debug-state guards; PC selection can use protected EPC/DPC based solely on decoded return signals.
inspection_records.notes: The csr_regfile-local evidence lacks a debug_mode_q guard for DRET and privilege guards for MRET/SRET.

579:             // trap to machine mode

581:                 // update mstatus
582:                 mstatus_d.mie  = 1'b0;

584:                 // save the previous privilege mode

603:         // ------------------------------
604:         // Debug
605:         // ------------------------------
606:         // Explains why Debug Mode was entered.
607:         // When there are multiple reasons to enter Debug Mode in a single cycle, hardware should set cause to the cause with the highest priority.
608:         // 1: An ebreak instruction was executed. (priority 3)
609:         // 2: The Trigger Module caused a breakpoint exception. (priority 4)
610:         // 3: The debugger requested entry to Debug Mode. (priority 2)
611:         // 4: The hart single stepped because step was set. (priority 1)
612:         // we are currently not in debug mode and could potentially enter
613:         if (!debug_mode_q) begin
614:             dcsr_d.prv = priv_lvl_o;
615:             // trigger module fired
616: 
617:             // caused by a breakpoint
618:             if (ex_i.valid && ex_i.cause == riscv::BREAKPOINT) begin
619:                 // check that we actually want to enter debug depending on the privilege level we are currently in
620:                 unique case (priv_lvl_o)
621:                     riscv::PRIV_LVL_M: begin
622:                         debug_mode_d = dcsr_q.ebreakm;
623:                         set_debug_pc_o = dcsr_q.ebreakm;
624:                     end

626:                         debug_mode_d = dcsr_q.ebreaks;
627:                         set_debug_pc_o = dcsr_q.ebreaks;
628:                     end
629:                     riscv::PRIV_LVL_U: begin
